remember two things:

   x + 6 ≥ 0  ⇒  x ≥ –6.
2. The square‐root symbol denotes the principal (non-negative) root, so √(…) is always ≥ 0.  
   Thus the right-hand side x must itself be ≥ 0.

Therefore any legitimate solution must satisfy x ≥ 0.

a) Test x = –2  
   • Domain check: –2 < 0, so it already fails the “x ≥ 0” requirement.  
   • Substitution: √(–2 + 6) = √4 = 2, but the right-hand side is –2.  
   They are not equal.  
   ⇒ x = –2 is not a solution.

b) Test x = 3  
   • Domain check: 3 ≥ 0, so it is permitted.  
   • Substitution: √(3 + 6) = √9 = 3, which equals the right-hand side.  
   ⇒ x = 3 satisfies the equation.

Answer:  
a) No, x = –2 is not a solution.  
b) Yes, x = 3 is a solution.
